description_long: This object consists of three AK-style rifles arranged in parallel, each featuring a distinct finish: one with a traditional dark wood and black metal, another with a gold-plated receiver and magazine, and the third with a more muted, matte metallic finish. The rifles have classic fixed wooden stocks, curved detachable box magazines, and standard-length barrels with front post iron sights. The gold-plated rifle stands out with its reflective, polished surfaces, while the others have a more utilitarian, matte appearance. All rifles display visible trigger guards, charging handles, and gas tubes above the barrels. The overall construction suggests robust use of metal and wood, typical of AK-pattern rifles, with each rifle exhibiting detailed mechanical components such as safety selectors and magazine releases. The arrangement and finishes make these rifles visually striking and easily recognizable in an urban context.
description: A set of three AK-style rifles, each with distinct finishes—traditional wood and black, gold-plated, and matte metal—featuring fixed wooden stocks, curved magazines, and standard iron sights.
description_view_0: Three AK-style rifles side by side, showing their full profiles and distinct finishes, with stocks and barrels clearly visible.
description_view_1: Front-facing view highlighting the muzzles, front sights, and the curvature of the magazines, with less emphasis on the stocks.
description_view_2: Reverse side view displaying the backs of the stocks and the right-side details of the receivers and magazines.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the alignment of the rifles, the width of the stocks, and the arrangement of the magazines.
